rulings.Case Title: PETER GALASSI et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 1, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is based on the sophisticated user defense as articulated in Johnson v. American Standard, Inc. (2008) 43 Cal.4th 56. "Under the sophisticated user defense, the inquiry focuses on whether the plaintiff knew, or should have known, of the particular risk of harm from the product giving rise to the injury." (Id. at 915.) Defendant's evidence is set forth in its' Separate Statement of Facts 2-4. The evidence generally describes jobs held by plaintiff and the completion of a four-year apprenticeship program to become a marine machinist. The record is devoid of evidence that would enable a trier of fact to conclude that plaintiff "knew, or should have known, of the particular risk of harm" from defendant's product or products of a similar kind at the time defendant was exposed to the product. The Johnson decision does not hold that the sophistication of plaintiff's employer is all that need be shown. The motion is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: PETER GALASSI et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 1, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is based on the sophisticated user defense as articulated in Johnson v. American Standard, Inc. (2008) 43 Cal.4th 56. "Under the sophisticated user defense, the inquiry focuses on whether the plaintiff knew, or should have known, of the particular risk of harm from the product giving rise to the injury." (Id. at 915.) Defendant's evidence is set forth in its' Separate Statement of Facts 5-8. The evidence generally describes jobs held by plaintiff, the completion of a four-year apprenticeship program "to give [plaintiff] a feel for everything." The record is devoid of evidence that would enable a trier of fact to conclude that plaintiff "knew, or should have known, of the particular risk of harm" from defendant's product or products of a similar kind at the time plaintiff was exposed to defendant's product. The Johnson decision does not hold that the sophistication of plaintiff's employer is all that need be shown. The motion is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY M PARKER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 1, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT FLUOR CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is denied. It is premised on demonstrating that defendant was not present at the Standard Oil/Chevron Refinery in Richmond. However, Mr. Smith's Declaration fails to meet the requirement of Evidence Code 1272 that the records reviewed were made in the regular course of business and are trustworthy. As a consequence, defendant has failed to shift the burden of proof. Assuming the burden was shifted, the declarations of Parker, Mingram and Ay construed in favor of plaintiff are sufficient to create a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. SEIU LOCAL 790-DONISE MANCHESTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate And/Or Correct Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 1, 2008, line 15. PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S Motion To Vacate And/Or Correct Arbitration Award. The award is vacated. The parties are to meet and confer in good faith to seek alternatives to respondent's layoff. The award is contrary to Article 9 of the M O U and as a consequence, the remedy fashioned exceeds the power of the arbitrator. (C C P 1286.2) Advanced Muro Devices, Inc. v. Intel Corp. (1994) 9 Cal. 4th 362, 383; Pacific Motor Trucking Co. v. Automotive Machinists (9th Cir. 1983) 702F.2d 176; IBEW, Local 175 v. Thomas & Betts Corp. (6th Cir. 1999) 182F. 3d 469, 472. The decision in Social Services Union et al v. Alameda County, et al (1989) 207 Cal. App. 3d 1458 is not controlling for the remedy was consistent with the M O U. The arbitrator found that petitioner violated the meet and confer provisions of the M O U and the appropriate remedy is to direct the parties to meet and confer and, in good faith, seek alternatives to respondents layoff. =(302/PJM/PB)
